question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause the Spring Data JPA repository to use a stale EntityManager that has cached entity state from before the partition, causing findRuntimeBytecode() to return bytecode from the first-level JPA cache rather than the DB, bypassing the Caffeine cache's TTL and serving indefinitely stale data?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause the web3 API to serve different bytecode versions for the same contract depending on whether the request hits a cached or non-cached code path, enabling the attacker to predict which path will be taken and craft requests that exploit the inconsistency for financial gain?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause the Caffeine cache to serve a runtime_bytecode that was valid at cache-population time but has since been replaced by a contract upgrade (UUPS/transparent proxy pattern), causing the EVM to execute the old implementation and bypass security fixes introduced in the upgrade?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause the DB connection pool validation query to fail, triggering HikariCP to mark all connections as invalid and attempt to create new ones simultaneously, causing a connection storm that overwhelms the DB when the partition recovers?
